purpose: Bubble Tea terminal UI for tg-digest: a two-tab (Dashboard / Sources) application that displays date-navigable AI-summarized content cards per source, supports per-source filtering, and orchestrates storage reads/writes plus optional refresh and summarization services.

entry_points: WithRefresh(svc RefreshService, autoRefresh bool, interval time.Duration) AppOption

config: autoRefresh bool and interval time.Duration (via WithRefresh)

cross_scope_touchpoints.surface: NewApp, WithRefresh, WithSummarize, AppOption — cmd/digest constructs and runs the App
refresh.RefreshResult (return type in RefreshService interface and refreshCompleteMsg)
summarizer.SummarizeQueueResult (return type in SummarizeService interface and summarizeCompleteMsg)

invariants: SourceFilter zero value matches all sources — callers and service methods both treat nil slice returns from MatchingChannelIDs/MatchingSourceNames as 'all'
When both SourceFilter.Types and SourceFilter.Names are set, Match requires both to pass (AND semantics) — the filter_overlay only ever produces Names-only filters, never Types+Names combinations
All storage operations happen inside tea.Cmd closures, never in Update — preserves the Elm architecture pure-function guarantee
filterOverlayModel.buildFilter returns an empty SourceFilter (not a full-selection Names filter) when all sources are selected — preserves the nil-means-all invariant for downstream services
risks: Sub-view commands are eagerly executed inline via result := cmd() in handleKeyMsg (app.go:285-303) rather than returned to the Bubble Tea runtime — breaks normal BubbleTea message routing and prevents middleware from intercepting loadSummariesMsg, deleteChannelMsg, and addSourceMsg

dashboard.expanded map is keyed by filtered-slice index; changing the active filter does NOT reset the map — previously expanded indices may refer to different sources after a filter change, causing incorrect card rendering (dashboard.go:143-154)

refreshFilteredCmd and summarizeFilteredCmd capture dashboard.filter and channels.channels at key-press time as stale value-type snapshots — filter state may diverge from channels if both were changing concurrently
Add-source wizard accepts whitespace-only source names: addNameInput is checked != '' but not trimmed (channels.go:145)
patterns: Functional options (AppOption) for optional RefreshService and SummarizeService — keeps NewApp minimal and testable without those capabilities
Elm architecture (Model/Update/View) via Bubble Tea; all side effects in tea.Cmd closures returned from Update

Service interfaces (RefreshService, SummarizeService) defined in the consumer package (tui/types.go) for dependency inversion — all other tui files are insulated from concrete refresh/summarizer packages
Package-level singleton keyMap (keys var in keys.go) — all key matching across all sub-models goes through this one instance
Centralized style constants in styles.go — only filter_overlay.go constructs a local style at render time (for dynamic width boxing)
Manual text-input handling (switch on msg.Type) in channels.go wizard steps instead of bubbles/textinput — simpler but no cursor positioning
Test integration uses a real SQLite database (t.TempDir) for storage; only RefreshService and SummarizeService are mocked
